author: Dr. Helena Vargas / Dr. Owen Pratt (Surgery PGY-3)
content:
ACUTE CARE SURGERY — DAILY PROGRESS NOTE
POD#1 s/p open cholecystectomy (6/10).

SUBJECTIVE: Resting comfortably. Pain controlled on scheduled acetaminophen with minimal oxycodone. Passing flatus. No nausea. Tolerated clear liquids overnight. No chest pain or shortness of breath.

OBJECTIVE:
- Afebrile since surgery (Tmax 99.1). HR/BP normal. (See vitals.)
- Telemetry: nursing noted SOME ATRIAL FIBRILLATION overnight — brief and self-limited; Medicine consulted to co-manage. Details documented in the telemetry event note (see NOTE004).
- Abdomen: incision clean/dry/intact, appropriate tenderness, drain with scant serosanguinous output. Bowel sounds present.
- Labs (LAB002): WBC downtrending to 11.2 from 17.6. Cr 0.9.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN (surgical):
#Perforated cholecystitis s/p open chole — improving. Continue pip/tazo; advance diet; remove drain when output low. Continue VTE prophylaxis (prophylactic enoxaparin started today) + SCDs.
#New telemetry atrial fibrillation — Medicine following; see their note. Continue telemetry.
#Pain — multimodal, opioid-sparing.
Anticipate discharge around POD#5 if course continues to improve.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Owen Pratt, MD (PGY-3) and Dr. Helena Vargas, MD (Acute Care Surgery). 6/11/2027.
